sense.definition: an educational unit that represents a completed course part
an official document that states one has successfully passed an exam or completed a course of study

examples.x: She received a certificate of completion after finishing the online training.
He framed his certificate and hung it on the wall in his office.
The certificate program is designed for working professionals looking to enhance their skills.
After passing the test, she was awarded a certificate in digital marketing.
You need a certificate in first aid to work as a lifeguard.

sense.definition: a certificate given to someone who has completed a course of study

examples.x: She proudly displayed her diploma on the wall of her office.
Graduates received their diplomas during the commencement ceremony.
He earned his high school diploma after completing four years of coursework.
After completing the apprenticeship program, he received a diploma in carpentry.
The diploma serves as proof of completion of the educational program and can be used for employment or further education.

sense.definition: an official certificate in the US that people who did not complete high school can obtain by taking some classes and successfully passing a test, which is the equivalent of the actual high school diploma

examples.x: He earned his General Equivalency Diploma after dropping out of high school.
The job required at least a General Equivalency Diploma for consideration.
She attended night classes to prepare for the General Equivalency Diploma test.
He was determined to get his General Equivalency Diploma to improve his career prospects.
The school offers resources for students pursuing a General Equivalency Diploma.

sense.definition: a qualification awarded by an educational institution, typically upon completion of a prescribed course of study

examples.x: She earned her academic degree in psychology after completing four years of undergraduate study.
His academic degree in computer science opened up many career opportunities in the technology industry.
The university offers a wide range of academic degrees, from bachelor's to doctoral programs.
To pursue her dream of becoming a teacher, she decided to pursue an academic degree in education.
His academic degree in business administration provided him with the knowledge and skills needed to succeed in the corporate world.

sense.definition: an academic degree awarded by colleges and universities upon completion of undergraduate studies

examples.x: After four years of hard work, she proudly received her baccalaureate in English literature.
The university offers various baccalaureate programs in fields such as biology, history, and economics.
He decided to pursue a baccalaureate in engineering to pursue his passion for innovation and problem-solving.
The baccalaureate degree provided her with a solid foundation for further studies or entry into the workforce.
She was excited to embark on her career path after earning her baccalaureate in business administration.

sense.definition: a two-year academic credential from a community college or technical school

examples.x: She earned her associate degree in computer science.
After completing his associate degree, he transferred to a four-year university.
The program offers associate degrees in business, healthcare, and engineering.
He decided to pursue an associate degree in culinary arts.
Many students start with an associate degree before pursuing higher education or entering the workforce.

sense.definition: the first degree given by a university or college to a student who has finished their studies
a university degree that graduates can get by further studying for one or two years
the highest degree given by a university

examples.x: She earned her doctorate in physics from Harvard University.
He was awarded a doctorate in philosophy after completing his dissertation.
The professor holds a doctorate in economics from Oxford University.
She decided to pursue a doctorate in literature to further her academic career.
After obtaining her doctorate, she joined the faculty as an assistant professor at a prestigious university.

sense.definition: a qualification awarded to students in South Africa who have achieved the necessary academic standards to proceed to tertiary education

examples.x: With her matric exemption, she was eligible to apply for admission to university.
Students who obtain a matric exemption have demonstrated proficiency in key subjects.
His matric exemption opened doors to various academic and career opportunities.
The university requires applicants to have a matric exemption to be considered for admission.
She worked hard throughout high school to achieve her matric exemption and pursue her dream career.

sense.definition: a provider of vocational qualifications in the United Kingdom, offering a range of courses and certifications in various subjects
a prize or money given to a person for their great performance

examples.x: She received a lifetime achievement award for her work in cinema.
She won an award for her exceptional performance in the film.
The book won several literary awards last year.
The film director received an award at the international film festival.
The student received an award for his outstanding academic achievements.

sense.definition: a physical object or award given to recognize achievements or contributions

examples.x: She proudly displayed her honor, a gleaming trophy, on the mantelpiece.
He wore his honor, a prestigious medal, with pride at the ceremony.
The prestigious literary award was considered a great honor among writers and poets.
The family heirloom, an ancient sword, was a cherished honor passed down through generations.
Receiving the academic honor of a gold medal was a testament to her exceptional achievements and scholarly dedication.

sense.definition: an academic qualification awarded for outstanding achievement in a higher education program

examples.x: She graduated with an honors degree in biology, earning recognition for her exceptional research.
His honors degree in economics opened doors to prestigious job opportunities in the finance sector.
His honors degree in economics opened doors to prestigious job opportunities in the finance sector.

sense.definition: an academic distinction awarded based on a student's exceptional performance

examples.x: As a recipient of the Latin honor cum laude, he was invited to join the university's honors society, recognizing his scholarly accomplishments.

sense.definition: (in the US) with the third highest level of distinction achievable by a student
(in the US) with the second highest level of distinction achievable by a student
(in the US) with the highest level of distinction achievable by a student
an academic qualification in British education awarded to students who achieve the highest level of academic excellence in their field of study
an academic classification in British education awarded to graduates who achieve a level of academic performance below that of a first class honours degree

examples.x: She graduated with a second class honours degree in Economics.
Despite challenges, she earned a second class honours degree in History.
Graduates with second class honours degrees are often recognized in the alumni magazine.
After earning his second class honours degree in Computer Science, he secured a position at a leading technology company.
Employers value candidates with second class honours degrees for their academic achievement.

sense.definition: an academic classification in British education awarded to graduates who achieve a level of academic performance below that of a second class honours degree

examples.x: Graduates with third class honours degrees are encouraged to explore various career opportunities.
After completing his third class honours degree in Business Administration, he pursued further education to enhance his skills.
Though not as common as higher classifications, third class honours degrees still demonstrate academic accomplishment.
